titleEn: Salamanca will host the ICED 2026 Conference
excerptEn: The Spanish University Teaching Network (REDU) and the University of Salamanca are pleased to announce that the ICED 2026 Conference will be held from 24 to 26 June 2026 in the city of Salamanca. Under the theme “Agency and Academic Development”, the conference aims to promote interdisciplinary reflection and dialogue…
contentEn:
The Spanish University Teaching Network (REDU) and the University of Salamanca are pleased to announce that the ICED 2026 Conference will be held from 24 to 26 June 2026 in the city of Salamanca.

Under the theme “Agency and Academic Development”, the conference aims to promote interdisciplinary reflection and dialogue on the many ways in which academic development can influence learning, teaching and institutional strategies at different levels.

The programme will be structured around three main thematic strands:

  1. Agency and knowledge: epistemologies, innovation and knowledge development.
  2. Agency and strategy: improving teaching and learning, and organisational transformation.
  3. Agency and students: student empowerment and curricular transformation.

The event will also feature a Pre-Conference Day on 23 June, and is aimed at academic development professionals, teaching and research staff, and anyone interested in sharing experiences, presenting research results and exploring innovative approaches in the university sphere.

The conference will be held in a hybrid format and in three languages: English, French and Spanish, thereby facilitating international participation.

Further information on registration, the programme and participation will be available soon. We look forward to welcoming you to Salamanca in June 2026!

excerptEn: Between 13 March and 23 May 2025, a range of educational and outreach initiatives will take place, designed to enrich the experience of visitors to the exhibition Flower in a Garden of Dreams: the University of Salamanca's collection of artificial models for teaching botany. Among the…
contentEn:
Between 13 March and 23 May 2025, a range of educational and outreach initiatives will take place, designed to enrich the experience of visitors to the exhibition Flower in a Garden of Dreams: the University of Salamanca's collection of artificial models for teaching botany.

Among the various activities, which you can consult in the information document included at the end of this text, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca hosted the seminars ‘Invisible pioneers: women and the visual representation of plants in the early steps of botany as a scientific discipline’ and ‘Communicating botany and heritage through scientific illustration’, delivered by David Martín Rubio on 13 and 14 March respectively.

If you would like to find out about the other activities and register, you can consult the following document:

titleEn: CONFERENCE ON RESEARCH METHODOLOGY IN EDUCATION
excerptEn: Organised by the Innovation Group in Educational Research Methodology (GRIMEINED) in collaboration with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), the Faculty of Education and the Department of Didactics, Organisation and Research Methods, on 6 and 7 February 2025 the 7th Conference on Research Methodology in…
contentEn:
Organised by the Innovation Group in Educational Research Methodology (GRIMEINED) in collaboration with the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), the Faculty of Education and the Department of Didactics, Organisation and Research Methods, on 6 and 7 February 2025 the 7th Conference on Research Methodology in Education took place.

During JOMEINED, around 50 education professionals shared their innovative teaching experiences in the training of tomorrow's education professionals. As the event was held in a hybrid format, professionals from Honduras, Chile and Costa Rica took part on Thursday afternoon. The first day concluded with the opening lecture, From PISA to the classroom: educational innovation for school equity. The EVIDENCE project, delivered by Professor Fernando Martínez Abad.

On Friday, after the opening ceremony, in which Susana Olmos Migueláñez took part as director of the IUCE, and the on-site working sessions, attendees had the opportunity to enjoy the presentation of the game Spearman-Speed, a card game designed to teach correlations. To round off Friday afternoon's session, attendees were able to enjoy an exhibition of the work produced by students in the Research Methodology module during the past academic year.

See you at JOMEINED26.

contentEn:

titleEn: Lecture: The influence of the social and physical environment surrounding viewers on their viewing experience
excerptEn: On 13 January 2025, the Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) of the University of Salamanca is holding the lecture entitled “The influence of the social and physical environment surrounding viewers on their viewing experience”, at 10.30 am in the Salón de Grados of the FES Building of the University of Salamanca. The lecture will be…
contentEn:
On 13 January 2025, the Audiovisual Content Observatory (OCA) of the University of Salamanca is holding the lecture entitled “The influence of the social and physical environment surrounding viewers on their viewing experience”, at 10.30 am in the Salón de Grados of the FES Building of the University of Salamanca.

The lecture will be delivered by Dr Nurit Tal-Or, associate professor in the Department of Communication at the University of Haifa (Israel) and currently a visiting researcher at the OCA, where she works with Professor Juanjo Igartua, director of the Observatory. This talk will explore how the physical and social environment in which we consume audiovisual media influences our viewing experience, covering aspects such as identification with characters, narrative transportation and other contextual factors.

Further information and event registration: https://eventum.usal.es/127443/detail/conferencia-the-influence-of-the-social-and-physical-environment-surrounding-viewers-on-their-viewi.html

Aimed at students, teachers, researchers and education professionals, we invite you to take part as an attendee or as an author by submitting a contribution. contentEn:
The organising committee of JOMEINED25 has extended until 23 December the deadline for receiving proposals for the 7th Conference on Research Methodology in Education.

Aimed at students, teachers, researchers and education professionals, we invite you to take part as an attendee or as an author by submitting a contribution.

JOMEINED25: https://diarium.usal.es/jomeined/

  • Salamanca, 6 and 7 February 2025 (on-site and online)
  • Exhibition of student work and innovative teaching experiences.
  • Thematic strands on innovation in MIDE teaching and in the training of education professionals.
  • Contributions in extended abstract format (max. 2,500 words) by 8 December (DEADLINE EXTENDED TO 23 DECEMBER)
  • Reduced fee for students.
  • Publications associated with the event.
  • Further information on the website or by email: jomeined@usal.es

titleEn: The VisualMed Systems group receives an award in the 4th edition of the ENNOVA HEALTH awards for the creation of a technological system for monitoring muscle fatigue in the operating theatre, in the IoT in E-Health, Wearables and Robotics category
excerptEn: Professor Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, head of the VisualMed Systems research group, affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences, together with Jaime Solleiro Rodríguez, a student on the “Education in the Knowledge Society” doctoral programme, received the Ennova Health Award, presented in Madrid on 19 November 2024, for the work entitled…
contentEn:
Professor Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, head of the VisualMed Systems research group, affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences, together with Jaime Solleiro Rodríguez, a student on the “Education in the Knowledge Society” doctoral programme, received the Ennova Health Award, presented in Madrid on 19 November 2024, for the work entitled: Personal, integrated auxiliary device for the monitoring and mitigation of muscle fatigue in the surgical setting.

This work forms part of the line of research being pursued for the preparation of his doctoral thesis.

The project also benefits from the collaboration of Professor Fernando Blaya Haro, of the Department of Mechanical Engineering and Industrial Design of the Technical University of Madrid; as well as Professor Francisco Javier de Toro Santos, Head of the Rheumatology Department at the Hospital, and professor at the Faculty of Physiotherapy of the University of A Coruña.

Also worthy of note is the participation in the clinical trials of the technological development of Alonso Blaya San Pedro, of the Centre for Bioengineering, Posture and Health S.L. in Madrid. Our research work aims to resolve, to a large extent, the main ergonomic problems that may be encountered in an operating theatre environment. To this end, we have developed an integrated device that makes it possible to control a surface electromyography unit, for the collection of muscle fatigue data, with a Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation device, responsible for applying a relaxing wave to the fatigued muscle, and to control the interaction between the two devices.

titleEn: Representatives of the PUCV visit the University of Salamanca
excerptEn: On Tuesday 16 September, representatives of the Pontifical Catholic University of Valparaíso (Chile) visited the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the Equality Unit of the University of Salamanca. Jimena Pascual, director of the School of Industrial Engineering and coordinator of the Chilean InES Género project “Reducing gender gaps…
contentEn:
On Tuesday 16 September, representatives of the Pontifical Catholic University of Valparaíso (Chile) visited the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the Equality Unit of the University of Salamanca.

Jimena Pascual, director of the School of Industrial Engineering and coordinator of the Chilean InES Género project “Reducing gender gaps in academia”, together with Patricia Jiménez, Coordinator of Quality Management and Teaching Innovation at the School of Industrial Engineering, took part in various meetings aimed at learning about the opportunities and challenges faced when defining and developing equality plans in the context of the University of Salamanca.

The InES Género Project of the Pontifical Catholic University of Valparaíso seeks to reduce gender gaps in the field of research, development and innovation through the implementation of a Development Plan for the promotion of gender equity in CTCI at the PUCV. InES is funded by the National Research and Development Agency of Chile and is run by the Vice-Rectorate for Research, Creation and Innovation. Further information at https://investigadoraspucv.cl/index.php/proyecto-ines-de-genero/.

titleEn: Conference on Women and citizen science, generating value in the territory of Castilla y León
excerptEn: On 27 September 2024 the Annual Meeting and Assembly of the Castilla y León Node of the Association of Women Researchers and Technologists (AMIT) will be held (https://amit-es.org/nodos_y_grupos/castilla-y-leon/). The conference, entitled “Women and citizen science, generating value in the territory of Castilla y León”, will take place in Salamanca, in the Aula de Grados…
contentEn:
On 27 September 2024 the Annual Meeting and Assembly of the Castilla y León Node of the Association of Women Researchers and Technologists (AMIT) will be held (https://amit-es.org/nodos_y_grupos/castilla-y-leon/).

The conference, entitled “Women and citizen science, generating value in the territory of Castilla y León”, will take place in Salamanca, in the Aula de Grados of the Solís Building of the Faculty of Education of the University of Salamanca, with the support of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) and the Equality Unit of the University of Salamanca.

The morning session is open to the general public from 12.00 until 14.00, after which attendees will be offered an aperitif. The opening of the event will feature Susana Olmos, director of the IUCE, Inmaculada Sánchez Barrios, director of the Equality Unit, and Patricia Sánchez Holgado, co-president of AMIT Castilla y León.

The opening lecture will be given by Mar Siles Luces, delegate of the CSIC in Castilla y León, followed by a round table with participants from various women and citizen science initiatives:

  • Micromundo: everyone against antibiotic resistance. Beatriz Santos Romero and Margarita Díaz Martínez (Institute of Functional Biology and Genomics, University of Salamanca)
  • HerStory: Combating gender stereotypes by highlighting women's contribution to the history of societies. Alicia García Holgado (Faculty of Sciences, University of Salamanca)
  • Citizen AI. Communicating Artificial Intelligence from a gender perspective. Carlos Arcila Calderón (Faculty of Social Sciences, University of Salamanca)
  • Social Action Legal Clinic – Life stories with a name of their own: an active listening process to transform reality through the law. Antonia Durán Ayago (Faculty of Law, University of Salamanca)

Finally, the closing session will be led by María Almudena Espinel, co-president of AMIT Castilla y León, Myriam Rodríguez López of Salamanca City Council and Sara Sánchez Hernández of Salamanca Provincial Council.

Registration completely free of charge https://forms.gle/dRnPqGEACSmL3h9Q7. Further information at cylamit@gmail.com.

titleEn: 11th edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences, IUCE, of the University of Salamanca is collaborating in the eleventh edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training (PIIECYL 2024) within the Community of Castilla y León, corresponding to the 2023-2024 academic year. The awards were announced by…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences, IUCE, of the University of Salamanca is collaborating in the eleventh edition of the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training (PIIECYL 2024) within the Community of Castilla y León, corresponding to the 2023-2024 academic year.

The awards were announced by Order EDU/1083/2023, of 30 August. The exhibition of the projects competing for the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training took place at the Hospedería Fonseca of the University of Salamanca on Tuesday 14 May 2024.

The works submitted will be published in a proceedings volume edited by the IUCE and available in open access, together with the proceedings of previous editions, at https://piiecyl.usal.es/comunicaciones-al-congreso/.

Full information on the award winners at https://www.salamanca24horas.com/local/estudiantes-salamanca-se-alzan-con-xi-premios-investigacion-innovacion-en-educacion-convoca-junta_15113825_102.html and the speech by the Regional Minister for Education of Castilla y León at https://www.salamanca24horas.com/universidad/rocio-lucas-destaca-papel-investigacion-en-aulas-comunidad-trabajamos-educacion-con-calidad-excelencia_15113812_102.html.

titleEn: Visit by Professor Marco Villalta of the University of Santiago de Chile
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca has received a visit from Professor Marco Antonio Villalta Paúcar, a lecturer at the School of Psychology of the University of Santiago de Chile (USACH). He was welcomed by the director of the IUCE, Professor Susana Olmos Migueláñez, and by Professor…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca has received a visit from Professor Marco Antonio Villalta Paúcar, a lecturer at the School of Psychology of the University of Santiago de Chile (USACH).

He was welcomed by the director of the IUCE, Professor Susana Olmos Migueláñez, and by Professor Juan Pablo Hernández Ramos, a collaborator on several of the projects that the Chilean university's academic has carried out in recent years, such as the FONDECYT 1200106 project.

Among the many matters addressed during Marco Villalta's visit, the possibility was established of consolidating the existing working relationship and opening up new collaborative research projects. Likewise, the possibility arises of hosting students from the USACH and of undertaking doctoral and predoctoral research stays at that university, located in the capital of Chile.

titleEn: 2023 E-nnova Health Award in the Telehealth and E-learning category
excerptEn: On 20 November 2023, at the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid, the 2023 E-nnova Health Award in the Telehealth and E-learning category was presented to Professor Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, director of the VisualMed Systems research group, affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences, jointly with Professor Dr F. Javier…
contentEn:
On 20 November 2023, at the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid, the 2023 E-nnova Health Award in the Telehealth and E-learning category was presented to Professor Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, director of the VisualMed Systems research group, affiliated with the University Institute of Education Sciences, jointly with Professor Dr F. Javier del Toro Santos, of the University of A Coruña, Rheumatology Department, A Coruña University Hospital, together with the collaboration of other physicians from the same centre, among them Jenaro Graña Gil, M Carmen San José Méndez and Roberto Rilo Antelo. In addition, Rafael Martín Holguera and Ana Isabel Turrión Nieves, of the University of Salamanca, have collaborated on this project.

The award-winning technological development was created by ARSoft, a company based in the Science Park of the University of Salamanca.

The award-winning technological procedure consists of an interactive simulation, using immersive virtual reality, in which participants experience practising clinical management and decision-making with a person presenting with monoarthritis in a medical consultation.

titleEn: 4th International Conference on Social and Productive Innovation 2023 (IV-CIISP2023)
excerptEn: The IV-CIISP2023 is a biennial event organised by the Vice-Rectorate for Research and Research Groups of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University, Peru, and the GRIAL Research Group of the University of Salamanca. The IV-CIISP2023 is a space for interaction and the dissemination of scientific and technological knowledge on society, the environment…
contentEn:
The IV-CIISP2023 is a biennial event organised by the Vice-Rectorate for Research and Research Groups of the Pedro Ruiz Gallo National University, Peru, and the GRIAL Research Group of the University of Salamanca.

The IV-CIISP2023 is a space for interaction and the dissemination of scientific and technological knowledge on society, the environment and economies, in order to attract interesting contributions arising from research work related to social and productive innovation.

THEMATIC PANELS

Social innovation

  • A.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for social innovation
  • A.2. Innovation in the field of health
  • A.3. Innovation in the field of education
  • A.4. Innovation and personal, family, workplace and community wellbeing
  • A.5. Innovation and the protection, improvement and conservation of the environment.
  • A.6. Innovation and land-use planning

Productive innovation

  • B.1. Capacities and entrepreneurship for productive innovation
  • B.3. Scientific and technological research and the protection of intellectual property.
  • B.4. Innovative national and international businesses and innovation in business
  • B.5. Innovative tourism.
  • B.6. Evaluation and promotion of productive innovation

REGISTRATION LINK TO ATTEND IV-CIISP2023: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1r8YLmPgeqgzIVuXkbZ-pWQMBsrp_ZOmyo_vsamJ0zrI/edit

GENERAL COORDINATION AND ENQUIRIES: Hilda Angélica Del Carpio Ramos, hdelcarpio@unprg.edu.pe

Information available in PDF.

titleEn: Iberaval Award for the Best ICT Project 2023 for the VisualMedSystems group and ARSoft, a company from the USAL Science Park
excerptEn: Iberaval is a mutual guarantee society which focuses on providing funding on better terms, in both repayment periods and pricing, to SMEs, self-employed workers and entrepreneurs with viable business projects. The project recognised by Iberaval falls within the framework of the STATE PROGRAMME FOR RESEARCH, DEVELOPMENT AND INNOVATION ORIENTED TOWARDS THE CHALLENGES OF SOCIETY, WITHIN THE FRAMEWORK OF THE STATE PLAN FOR…
contentEn:
Iberaval is a mutual guarantee society which focuses on providing funding on better terms, in both repayment periods and pricing, to SMEs, self-employed workers and entrepreneurs with viable business projects.

The project recognised by Iberaval falls within the framework of the STATE PROGRAMME FOR RESEARCH, DEVELOPMENT AND INNOVATION ORIENTED TOWARDS THE CHALLENGES OF SOCIETY, WITHIN THE FRAMEWORK OF THE STATE PLAN FOR SCIENTIFIC AND TECHNICAL RESEARCH AND INNOVATION, and the project is entitled: Automatic 3D modelling system for health.

The NextMed platform is a solution developed jointly by the company ARsoft and the recognised research group Visual Med Systems of the University of Salamanca led by Professor Juan A. Juanes Méndez, which improves the health system thanks to the use of new technologies such as, in this case, Augmented, Virtual and Mixed Reality (XR technologies).

The awards will be presented at a gala to be held on 19 October 2023 at 7.00 pm in the Business Solutions building in Arroyo de la Encomienda.

titleEn: NextMed receives an award at the Auggie Awards 2023
excerptEn: The NextMed project has once again received an award at the prestigious annual Auggie Awards of the Augmented World Expo (AWE) 2023, held in Santa Clara, California, USA, on 1 June, in the “Best healthcare and wellness solution” category. The VisualMed System research group and Arsoft are taking part in this project. Santiago…
contentEn:
The NextMed project has once again received an award at the prestigious annual Auggie Awards of the Augmented World Expo (AWE) 2023, held in Santa Clara, California, USA, on 1 June, in the “Best healthcare and wellness solution” category.

The VisualMed System research group and Arsoft are taking part in this project. 

Santiago González Izard, CEO of ARSOFT, accompanied by part of his team, collected the award.

This year the Auggie Awards reached their fourteenth edition, with a record number of nominations submitted, finalists selected and winners announced.

The awards jury highlighted the high standard of the nominations submitted in all categories and recognised the work that ARSOFT has been carrying out in the field of medicine in recent years. Specifically, the award went to the development of the NextMed platform, a research project produced together with the VisualMed System group of the University of Salamanca,  led by Professor Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez. A solution that improves the health system thanks to the use of new technologies such as Augmented, Virtual and Mixed Reality (XR technologies).

The NextMed platform is designed for the study of medical images using XR technologies. Through segmentation and the automatic generation of 3D models, NextMed helps medical professionals to study and manipulate the 3D model of the organs on which they have to operate. In this way, and thanks to the three-dimensional perspective, the surgeon's precision and confidence when facing an operation are increased, as the system helps them to understand what their patients' structures and tissues are like.

titleEn: 2022 E-nnova Health Award for the VisualMed Systems group
excerptEn: The Institute's research group VisualMed Systems, led by Professor Juan A. Juanes, together with the company ARSoft, of the Science Park of the University of Salamanca, have been honoured with the E-nnova Health Award for the best initiatives in digital health, in the Digital transformation category, for the NextMed research project,…
contentEn:
The Institute's research group VisualMed Systems, led by Professor Juan A. Juanes, together with the company ARSoft, of the Science Park of the University of Salamanca, have been honoured with the E-nnova Health Award for the best initiatives in digital health, in the Digital transformation category, for the NextMed research project, on automatic 3D modelling systems for health. A project oriented towards the challenges of society, within the framework of the state plan for scientific and technical research and innovation. The NextMed project is a novel Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR) system for medical and surgical use, which enables the spatial viewing, in three dimensions, of body structures generated from radiological images.

Further information: https://www.diariomedico.com/profesion/e-nnova-health-2022-premio-las-mejores-iniciativas-en-salud-digital.html

titleEn: 4th Conference on the Scientific Heritage of the University of Salamanca
excerptEn: The 4th Conference on the Scientific Heritage of the University of Salamanca will take place on Thursday 25 May 2023, from 9.30 am, at the University Institute of Education Sciences and will be streamed live on the YouTube channel of the Faculty of Social Sciences https://www.youtube.com/@sumas-sociales. The 4th Conference is structured…
contentEn:
The 4th Conference on the Scientific Heritage of the University of Salamanca will take place on Thursday 25 May 2023, from 9.30 am, at the University Institute of Education Sciences and will be streamed live on the YouTube channel of the Faculty of Social Sciences https://www.youtube.com/@sumas-sociales.

The 4th Conference is structured around two strands for action and debate:  identifying and describing good practices in safeguarding the scientific, technical and educational heritage of the University of Salamanca; and sharing experiences that generate positive changes in those practices with national or international networks.
